rule_text: A trader should execute volatility-selling trades when implied volatility significantly exceeds the expected realized volatility over the option's lifetime, accepting short-term drawdowns because profit derives from the volatility differential over time, not daily price moves.
entry_conditions: Implied volatility > forecast realized volatility by at least 15 percentage points
Initial vega exposure is at least $1,000 per contract
Expected profit from volatility differential = vega × (implied_vol − forecast_realized_vol) ≥ 2× max acceptable daily loss
exit_conditions: Cumulative loss exceeds 50% of initial expected vega profit
Implied volatility converges to within 5 points of realized volatility forecast
Time decay has captured 70% of initial vega value

rule_text: A trader should reduce position sizing when market noise exhibits fat tails (excess kurtosis), because optimal sizing formulas calibrated to normal distributions become too aggressive under non-normal conditions.
entry_conditions: Asset price deviates from fair value by N standard deviations (where N is calibrated entry threshold)
Kurtosis detection: Rolling 20-period excess kurtosis > 1.0 (moderate fat tails detected)
Commit initial position sizing at 25% of optimal formula result
exit_conditions: Asset returns to fair value (mean reversion exit)
Stop loss if position moves 3 standard deviations against entry
haiku_reason: The signal has specific, quantifiable entry conditions (price deviation by N standard deviations, rolling 20-period excess kurtosis > 1.0, 25% position sizing) and exit conditions (mean reversion, 3 standard deviation stop loss) that can be programmatically detected from market data.
